content: Most existing Self-Refine frameworks treat LLM self-feedback as a single consistent evaluation signal, but we can borrow the multi-perspective inconsistency principle from cutting-edge LLM self-reflection mechanisms to redesign Self-Refine: instead of asking the same LLM to generate feedback from a single fixed perspective each iteration, we prompt it to generate feedback from multiple inconsistent solving perspectives (e.g. end-user, code reviewer, security auditor) and contrast conflicting feedback to find overlooked flaws the single-perspective Self-Refine would miss. This adapted Self-Refine retains the core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision, while gaining the error detection benefits of multi-perspective reflection.
